(2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Ofi&cial Gazette. 2. In the Cable Television Networks Rules, 1994— (i) i n rale 6, in sub-rule (1) after clause (o) the following proviso shall be inserted, nam ely;— “ Provided that no film or film song or film promo or film trailer or music video or music albums or their promos, whether produced in India or abroad, shall be carried through cable service unless it has been certified by the Central Board of Film Certiflcafion (CBFC) as suhable for unrestricted public exhibition in India.” (ii) in rule 7, for sub-rale (9) the following sliall be substituted, namely :— “ (9) No advertisentent wliich \iolates tlie Code for self-regulation in advertising, as adopted by the Advertising Standard Council of India (ASCI), Mumbai for pubUc exhibition in India, liom tim e to time, shall be carried in the cable service. ” [F. No. 2302/1/2001 -BC-m (Part)] N. BAIJENDRA KUMAR, Jt. Sec}'. N ote: The principal rules werepublished in Gazette of India, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i) vide number GSR 729(E), dated the 29th September, 1994 and subsequently amended by GSR 459(E), dated the 8th October, 1996 and GSR 710(E), dated the 8th September, 2000 and GSR 282(E), dated the 1Itli May, 2006.
